name: Pion WebRTC
description: Pion — pure Go WebRTC implementation with extensible building blocks

seo.title: Pion WebRTC — Real-time / WebSocket Infrastructure
seo.meta_description: Pion WebRTC: Pion — pure Go WebRTC implementation with extensible building blocks

when_to_use: Building custom WebRTC applications in Go without C dependencies
WebRTC peer-to-peer for IoT device streaming in Go services
Implementing custom signaling and ICE for Go applications
Go-native WebRTC SFU/MCU building blocks for media servers
Unit-testable WebRTC stack for Go developers

pros: Pure Go WebRTC implementation with strong ergonomics
Great for building custom SFUs and media tools
Active maintenance and clean module layout
Good fit for cloud-native Go backends
cons: You build signaling, scaling and ops
Smaller ecosystem than C/C++ WebRTC stacks
Some advanced codec paths less optimized
review.verdict: Best WebRTC foundation for Go teams building custom real-time infrastructure from the ground up.
review.best_for: Go engineers writing custom SFUs, bots or media servers
review.not_for: Product teams that just want to drop in a video SDK
